author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.

ccel.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

title: Psalm CXII

source.title: NPNF1-08. St. Augustine: Exposition on the Book of Psalms

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: I believe, brethren, that ye remarked and committed to memory the title of this Psalm.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The conversion,” he saith, “of Haggai and Zechariah.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: These prophets were not as yet in existence, when these verses were sung.…But both, the one within a year after the other, began to prophesy that which seemeth to pertain to the restoration of the temple, as was foretold so long before.…“For the temple of God is holy, which temple ye are.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whoever therefore converteth himself to the work of this building together, and to the hope of a firm and holy edifice, like a living stone from the miserable ruin of this world, understandeth the title of the Psalm, understandeth “the conversion of Haggai and Zechariah.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let him therefore chant the following verses, not so much with the voice of his tongue as of his life.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the completion of the building will be that ineffable peace of wisdom, the “beginning” of which is the “fear of the Lord:” let him therefore, whom this conversion buildeth together, begin thence.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Blessed is the man that feareth the Lord: he will have great delight in His commandments” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: God, who alone judgeth both truthfully and mercifully, will see how far he obeyeth His commandments: since “the life of man on earth is a temptation,” as holy Job saith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But “He who judgeth us is the Lord.”…He therefore will see how far each man profiteth in His commandments; yet he who loveth the peace of this building together, shall have great delight in them; nor ought he to despair, since there is “peace on earth for men of good will.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Next follows, “His seed shall be mighty upon earth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The Apostle witnesseth, that the works of mercy are the seed of the future harvest, when he saith, “Let us not be weary in well doing, for in due season we shall reap;” and again, “But this I say, He which soweth sparingly, shall reap also sparingly.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But what, brethren, is more mighty than that not only Zacchæus should buy the kingdom of Heaven by the half of his goods, but even the widow for two mites, and that each should possess an equal share there?

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: What is more mighty, than that the same kingdom should be worth treasures to the rich man, and a cup of cold water to the poor?…“Glory and riches shall be in his house” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For his house is his heart; where, with the praise of God, he liveth in greater riches with the hope of eternal life, than with men flattering, in palaces of marble, with splendidly adorned ceilings, with the fear of everlasting death.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For his righteousness endureth for ever:” this is his glory, there are his riches.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: While the other’s purple, and fine linen, and grand banquets, even when present, are passing away; and when they have come to an end, the burning tongue shall cry out, longing for a drop of water from the finger’s end.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Unto the right-hearted there ariseth up light in the darkness” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Justly do the godly direct their heart unto their God, justly do they walk with their God, preferring His will to themselves; and having no proud presumption in their own.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they remember that they were some time in darkness, but are now light in the Lord.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Merciful, pitying, and just is the Lord God.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It delighteth us that He is “merciful and pitying,” but it perhaps terrifieth us that the Lord God is “just.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Fear not, despair not at all, happy man, who fearest the Lord, and hast great delight in His commandments; be thou sweet, be merciful and lend.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For the Lord is just in this manner, that He judgeth without mercy him who hath not shown mercy; but, “Sweet is the man who is merciful and lendeth” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 5): God will not spew him out of His mouth as if he were not sweet.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “Forgive,” He saith, “and ye shall be forgiven; give, and it shall be given unto you.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Whilst thou forgivest that thou mayest be forgiven, thou art merciful; whilst thou givest that it may be given unto thee, thou lendest.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For though all be called generally mercy where another is assisted in his distress, yet there is a difference where thou spendest neither money, nor the toil of bodily labour, but by forgiving what each man hath sinned against thee, thou gainest free pardon for thine own sins also.…He who is unwilling to give to the poor, seeketh riches; listen to what is written, “Thou shalt have treasure in heaven.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thou wilt not then lose honour by forgiving: for it is a very laudable triumph to conquer anger: wilt not grow poor by giving; for a heavenly treasure is a more safe possession.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: The former verse, “Riches and plenteousness shall be in his house,” was pregnant with this verse.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore who doth these things, “shall guide his words with discretion.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His deeds themselves are the words whereby he shall be defended at the Judgment; which shall not be without mercy unto him, since he hath himself shown mercy.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “For he shall never be moved” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 6): he who, called to the right hand, shall hear these words, “Come, ye blessed of My Father, inherit the kingdom prepared for you from the foundation of the world.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For no works of theirs, save works of mercy, are there mentioned.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore shall hear, “Come, ye blessed of My Father;” for, “the generation of the right ones shall be blessed.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Thus, “the righteous shall be had in everlasting remembrance.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He will not be afraid of any evil hearing; for his heart standeth fast and believeth in the Lord” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Such as the words which he will hear addressed to those on the left hand, “Depart into everlasting fire, prepared for the devil and his angels.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: He therefore who seeketh here not his own things, but those of Jesus Christ, most patiently endureth sufferings, waiteth for the promises with faith.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Nor is he broken down by any temptations: “His heart is established, and will not shrink, until he see beyond his enemies” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: His enemies wished to see good things here, and when invisible blessings were promised them, used to say, “Who will show us any good?”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: Let our heart therefore be established, and shrink not, until we see beyond our enemies.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For they wish to see good things of men in the land of the dying; we trust to see the good things of the Lord in the land of the living.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: But it is a great thing to have the heart established, and not to be moved, while they rejoice who love what they see, and mock at him who hopeth for what he seeth not; “what the Lord hath prepared for them that love Him.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: How great is the value of this which is not seen, and it is bought for so much as each man is able to give for it.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: On this account he also “dispersed abroad, and gave to the poor” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 9): he saw not, yet he kept buying; but He was storing up the treasure in heaven, who deigned to hunger and thirst in the poor on earth.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: It is no wonder then if “his righteousness remaineth for ever:” He who created the ages being his guardian.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “His horn,” whose humility was scorned by the proud, “shall be exalted with honour.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The ungodly shall see it, and he shall be angered” (ver.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: 10): this is that late and fruitless repentance.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For with whom rather than himself is he “angered,” when he shall say, “Our pride, what hath it profited us? the boastfulness of our riches, what hath it given us?” seeing the horn of him exalted with honour, who “dispersed abroad, and gave to the poor.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “He shall gnash with his teeth, and consume away:” for “there shall be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: For he will no more bring forth leaves and bloom, as would happen if he had repented in season: but he will then repent, when “the desire of the ungodly shall perish,” no consolation succeeding.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “The desire of the ungodly shall perish,” when “all things shall pass away like a shadow,” when the flower shall fall down on the withering of the grass.

verses.book: Expositions on the Book of Psalms.
verses.text: “But the word of the Lord that endureth for ever,” as it is mocked by the vanity of the falsely happy, so will laugh at the perdition of the same when truly miserable.
